sys_script_include.description:
2.7 STRY0099280 - DIA: Time in Task suspense date defaults to the time when creating the task. Adjust the default time to the end of the day for all new tasks. STRY0110317 - DIA: Time in Task suspense date defaults to the time when creating the Templates. Adjust the default time to the end of the day for all new Templates.
2.6 NOTE: The initial implementation uses a system property and all templates will use that schedule to block days. In this function we will be able to set the schedule based on any values passed in the current record. If we want to set it based on the user, we could look at the sys_user table to find their default schedule. If we want to use the Primary Office, Tasking Authority or Primary Assignee, we could set a default schedule and pull here. We could also allow each Template to set a schedule. Or, we could allow each Template Task to overide/set their own schedule. Finally, if we want to allow users to select a schedule when creating a schedule, we could grab it from there too.
sys_script_include.name: TaskerDurationCalculator
